w:r.w:t: Admin Panel + Worker Engine + Analytics Dashboard
4 Accounts · 500 Messages/Day · A/B Testing · Campaign Management
Micro-Detailed for AI Coding Agents — Zero Hallucination Build Guide
2. Exact Build Order (What to Code First)
3. Complete Tech Stack with Exact Versions
4. Prerequisites — Every Single Thing You Must Provide
5. Every Environment Variable for Both Apps
PART B: DATABASE (EXACT SQL — COPY-PASTE READY)
6. All 11 Tables with Full CREATE TABLE Statements
27. Dashboard Page — Every Card, Chart, Action
28. Campaign Manager — List, Create Wizard, Detail
45. A/B Testing Engine — Statistical Method & Code
This system has THREE independent components that communicate through a shared Supabase database. They never talk to each other directly.
Long-running Node.js process. Manages Baileys sockets, reads from message_queue, sends WhatsApp messages.
VPS (Oracle Free / AWS / Hetzner). Must be always-on.
Next.js 14 web application. Admin panel with campaigns, templates, analytics, settings.
Supabase (managed PostgreSQL). Stores all data. Provides Realtime subscriptions.
Supabase Cloud (free tier OK for dev).
Browser → Next.js API route → Supabase INSERT into campaigns + message_queue rows
Worker polls message_queue → locks row (status=processing) → resolves Spintax → stealth send via Baileys → UPDATE row to sent → INSERT into send_logs
Supabase Realtime pushes send_logs INSERT to Dashboard via WebSocket → KPI cards update instantly
Admin changes slider → PATCH /api/v1/config → UPDATE system_config → Supabase Realtime pushes to Worker → Worker updates in-memory config
⚠️ CRITICAL RULE: Worker and Dashboard NEVER communicate directly
No HTTP calls between them. No shared process. No WebSocket between them.
Supabase is the ONLY shared medium. This is intentional for reliability.
You can restart the worker without affecting the dashboard, and vice versa.
2. Exact Build Order (What to Code First)
Follow this order exactly. Each step depends on the previous one. Do NOT skip ahead.
Project setup: npm init, install packages, folder structure, .env files
Supabase: Run ALL SQL migrations (tables, indexes, functions, triggers, seed data)
Test: Pair all 4 accounts via QR code
Worker: src/index.js (main entry, ties everything together)
Test: Import 10 contacts, send test campaign, verify full loop
Dashboard: Next.js project setup, auth, layout, providers
Dashboard: Campaign manager (list + create wizard + detail)
Dashboard: Dashboard home (KPI cards + charts + live feed)
Integration test: Full campaign from dashboard to delivery
Deploy: Worker to VPS, Dashboard to Vercel
3. Complete Tech Stack with Exact Versions
WhatsApp Multi-Device protocol. Creates socket connections, sends messages, manages presence.
Supabase client. Used for all DB reads/writes and Realtime subscriptions.
Renders QR codes in terminal for WhatsApp pairing.
Structured JSON logger. Baileys requires pino.
Cron scheduler for daily reset and periodic tasks.
Parse CSV files for contact import.
Next.js framework. Use App Router, NOT Pages Router.
Supabase client for DB and Realtime.
Charts library. Used for all dashboard visualizations.
Schema validation. Shared between forms and API routes.
Data tables with sort, filter, pagination.
CSV parsing in the browser for contact uploads.

Do NOT npm install it. Initialize with: npx shadcn@latest init. Then add components one-by-one: npx shadcn@latest add button card input label select textarea tabs dialog sheet dropdown-menu table badge progress slider switch toast separator avatar scroll-area
4. Prerequisites — Every Single Thing You Must Provide
Hand this checklist to the person starting the project. Every unchecked item blocks progress.
Pick region closest to your VPS. Save the DB password.
Used in dashboard frontend (safe to expose).
Used in worker + Next.js server-side ONLY. Never expose.
Toggle ON for: send_logs, wa_accounts, message_queue, system_config, ab_variants
Create bucket named 'uploads'. Set to private.
Enable Email provider. Disable 'Confirm email' for dev.
Email + password. This is your dashboard login.
Physical SIMs in phones, or virtual numbers (e.g., TextNow, Google Voice)
Each number runs an independent Baileys session.
WhatsApp installed and registered on each
Open WhatsApp, verify the number, see the chat screen
Baileys pairs as a 'linked device' to an existing WhatsApp account.
Numbers should have been using WhatsApp for at least a month
Fresh accounts get flagged and banned much faster.
Set profile name, profile photo, and About text
Empty profiles trigger WhatsApp spam detection.
You only need the phone during initial pairing. After that, put it away.
Baileys links via QR. Once linked, the phone doesn't need to be online.
Oracle Cloud Free Tier (BEST free option)
NEVER deploy the Worker to Vercel, Netlify, or any serverless platform.
Baileys maintains persistent WebSocket connections that must stay alive for hours.
Serverless functions timeout after 10–60 seconds. The worker will crash immediately.
The Worker MUST run on an always-on VPS with PM2 or Docker.
Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → New Webhook → Copy URL
Gmail: smtp.gmail.com:587 + App Password. Or use SendGrid/Mailgun.
5. Every Environment Variable for Both Apps
Pino log level: debug, info, warn, error. Default: info
✅ Note: All Other Config is in the Database Now
In this version, timing, limits, webhook URLs, stealth parameters, etc.
are ALL stored in the system_config table and managed from the Settings page.
The worker's .env is minimal — just the Supabase connection.
5.2 Dashboard: .env.local (in Next.js project root, also set in Vercel)
Same Supabase URL. NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix exposes to browser.
Anon key. Safe for frontend. RLS enforced.
Service role. ONLY used in API routes (server-side).
6. All 11 Tables — Full CREATE TABLE SQL
Run every SQL block below in the Supabase SQL Editor, in the order shown. Each block is copy-paste ready.
13. Indexes → 14. Functions & Triggers → 15. Seed data → 16. RLS policies
Stores metadata and session data for each WhatsApp number.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
Counter. Reset to 0 at midnight by cron.
Max messages this account can send per day. Adjustable from dashboard.
Timestamp of last successfully sent message.
If set, worker skips this account until this time.
Baileys socket state. Updated by worker.
When the socket last successfully connected.
Baileys auth credentials. Synced after every creds.update event.
Central job queue. ONE row = ONE outbound message.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
campaign_id UUID REFERENCES campaigns(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
assigned_account_id UUID REFERENCES wa_accounts(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
variant_id UUID REFERENCES ab_variants(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
campaign_id and variant_id reference tables created later. Run this CREATE TABLE AFTER campaigns and ab_variants exist, OR create without the FK constraints first and add them later via ALTER TABLE.
Immutable audit trail. One row per send attempt (success or failure). Never UPDATE or DELETE rows here.
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
queue_item_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES message_queue(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
account_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES wa_accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
campaign_id UUID REFERENCES campaigns(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
variant_id UUID REFERENCES ab_variants(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('sent','failed')),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
send_window_start INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 10 CHECK (send_window_start BETWEEN 0 AND 23),
send_window_end INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 22 CHECK (send_window_end BETWEEN 0 AND 23),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
is_archived BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
auto_select_winner BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
experiment_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES ab_experiments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
template_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES message_templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
is_winner BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
is_blacklisted BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
7. Foreign Key Constraints (Run After All Tables Exist)
ALTER TABLE campaigns ADD CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_template
FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES message_templates(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
ALTER TABLE campaigns ADD CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_experiment
FOREIGN KEY (ab_experiment_id) REFERENCES ab_experiments(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
ALTER TABLE campaigns ADD CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_segment
FOREIGN KEY (segment_id) REFERENCES contact_segments(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
ALTER TABLE ab_experiments ADD CONSTRAINT fk_experiments_winner
FOREIGN KEY (winner_variant_id) REFERENCES ab_variants(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
Every index with its exact purpose. These are critical for query performance.
CREATE INDEX idx_queue_pending_scheduled ON message_queue(status, scheduled_for) WHERE status = 'pending';
Dispatcher query: find next message to send. Partial index keeps it small.
CREATE INDEX idx_queue_campaign ON message_queue(campaign_id, status);
Campaign progress: count sent/failed per campaign.
CREATE INDEX idx_queue_account ON message_queue(assigned_account_id) WHERE assigned_account_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_queue_variant ON message_queue(variant_id) WHERE variant_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_logs_created ON send_logs(created_at DESC);
Dashboard live feed: newest logs first.
CREATE INDEX idx_logs_account ON send_logs(account_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_logs_campaign ON send_logs(campaign_id, created_at DESC) WHERE campaign_id IS NOT NULL;
Dedupe on import. (UNIQUE already creates this, but explicit for clarity.)
CREATE INDEX idx_contacts_tags ON contacts USING GIN(tags);
GIN index for array contains queries on tags.
CREATE INDEX idx_templates_active ON message_templates(is_archived, created_at DESC) WHERE is_archived = false;
Template list: show non-archived, newest first.
Apply to every table that has an updated_at column.
CREATE TRIGGER trg_wa_accounts_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON wa_accounts
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER trg_campaigns_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON campaigns
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER trg_templates_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON message_templates
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER trg_segments_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON contact_segments
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
CREATE TRIGGER trg_config_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON system_config
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
Resets message counters at midnight. Call via Supabase cron or pg_cron.
-- If using pg_cron (enable in Supabase: Database > Extensions > pg_cron):
Run this INSERT to populate all default config values. The worker reads these on startup.
INSERT INTO system_config (key, value, description) VALUES
('global_send_enabled', 'true', 'Master kill switch. false = all sending paused.'),
('daily_total_limit', '500', 'Max messages per day across all accounts.'),
('presence_offline_min_sec', '5', 'Min seconds before going offline after send.'),
('presence_offline_max_sec', '15', 'Max seconds before going offline after send.'),
('alert_on_daily_miss', 'true', 'Alert if daily target not met by 9 PM.');
Create placeholder rows for your 4 WhatsApp numbers. Replace phone numbers with your actual numbers.
INSERT INTO wa_accounts (phone_number, display_name, status) VALUES
The Supabase client singleton. Every other file imports from here.
throw new Error('Missing SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY');
Loads all system_config rows into memory. Subscribes to Realtime for live updates.
Fetches ALL rows from system_config. Stores in a Map. Call once at startup.
Reads from in-memory Map. Returns defaultValue if key not found.
Subscribes to system_config changes via Supabase Realtime. Auto-updates the in-memory Map when a row changes.
system_config.value is JSONB. When you read getConfig('send_window_start'), the value is already a parsed JavaScript value (number 10, not string '10'). No need to JSON.parse in the consuming code.
Drop-in replacement for Baileys' useMultiFileAuthState. Syncs auth credentials to Supabase so sessions survive across machines.
Check if wa_accounts.session_data is non-null for this accountId.
If YES: Write session_data to local auth folder (auth/<accountId>/) as files.
If NO: Create empty auth folder. Baileys will generate new credentials (triggers QR).
Call Baileys' useMultiFileAuthState('auth/<accountId>') to get { state, saveCreds }.
Wrap saveCreds: after calling the original saveCreds, read all files from the auth folder, serialize to JSON, and UPDATE wa_accounts.session_data = <json> WHERE id = accountId.
The auth folder contains multiple files (creds.json, app-state-sync-key-*, etc.).
When restoring, iterate the object keys and write each file back to the folder.
Manages 4 simultaneous Baileys WebSocket connections. This is the core engine.
Initializes empty Map<accountId, { socket, account }>. Does NOT connect yet.
1. Query wa_accounts from Supabase. 2. For each account, call initSocket(account). 3. Log summary: '4 sockets initialized, 3 connected, 1 pairing'.

1. Call useSupabaseAuthState(account.id). 2. Create socket: makeWASocket({ auth: state, logger, browser: ['WA-Auto', 'Chrome', '4.0.0'] }). 3. Register event handlers. 4. Store in Map. 5. Return socket.
Filter Map: connection_status === 'connected' AND status === 'active' AND messages_sent_today < daily_limit.
From getHealthy(), sort by messages_sent_today ASC, then last_message_at ASC. Return first. Return null if none available.

Switch on update.connection: 'open' → set connected+active. 'close' → check lastDisconnect.error.statusCode. 401 → banned+alert. 408 → reconnect with backoff. 440 → needs re-pair+alert. 515 → reconnect after 5s.
Wait delayMs, then call initSocket again. Use exponential backoff: 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 60s.
For each socket: sock.end(). Clear the Map. Log 'All sockets closed'.
1. Update wa_accounts.status = 'banned'. 2. Delete session_data. 3. Send webhook alert: CRITICAL. 4. Do NOT auto-reconnect.
1. Update connection_status = 'reconnecting'. 2. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s). 3. Log warning.
Connection replaced (another Baileys instance connected with same creds)
1. Update status = 'disconnected'. 2. Send webhook alert: needs re-pair. 3. Do NOT auto-reconnect.
1. Update status = 'cooldown', cooldown_until = now + 30min. 2. Send webhook alert: HIGH.
1. Wait 5 seconds. 2. Auto-reconnect. 3. Log warning with full error object.
The anti-ban core. Executes a human-like behavior sequence before every single message.
* @param {WASocket} socket - The Baileys socket to use
async function executeStealth(socket, jid, messageText, config) { ... }
Always do this first. Makes the account appear online.
Only execute 30% of the time (configurable). Simulates checking inbox.
const result = await sock.sendMessage(jid, { text: messageText })
Don't await this. Fire-and-forget with setTimeout.
After step 6, the dispatcher must wait before processing the next message. Calculate:
* Resolves a Spintax template into a final message string.
* @param {string} template - e.g., '{Hello|Hi|Hey} [Name], {how are you|hope you are well}'
return variables[key] || match; // Keep original if no value
One of A, B, C, D, or E (equal probability)
{ allowed: bool, reason: string, sleepMs: number }
1. Is global_send_enabled true? 2. Is current hour within send_window? 3. Is total sent today < daily_total_limit? 4. Is at least one account available?
Checks blacklist table for this phone number.
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM send_logs WHERE status='sent' AND created_at >= today_midnight.
If getConfig('global_send_enabled') is false: return { allowed: false, reason: 'Sending paused by admin', sleepMs: 60000 }
Get current hour in configured timezone (send_timezone). If hour < send_window_start: return { allowed: false, reason: 'Before send window', sleepMs: msUntilWindowOpens }
If hour >= send_window_end: return { allowed: false, reason: 'After send window', sleepMs: msUntilTomorrowWindowOpens }
If getTodayTotalSent() >= daily_total_limit: return { allowed: false, reason: 'Daily limit reached', sleepMs: msUntilMidnight }
The main processing loop. Dequeues messages and orchestrates the entire send pipeline.
Stores reference to ClientManager. Sets running = false.
Sets running = true. Calls tick() in a loop with recursive setTimeout.
Sets running = false. Waits for current tick to finish.
The core algorithm. See exact steps below.
SELECT * FROM message_queue WHERE status='pending' AND scheduled_for <= now() ORDER BY scheduled_for ASC LIMIT 1
UPDATE message_queue SET status='processing' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'pending'
If 0 rows affected: another instance grabbed it. Skip, return.
If null: release row (status=pending), sleep 5min, send alert
If row.campaign has ab_experiment: selectVariant(). Else: use campaign template.
If error: use campaign's default template
If error: mark failed, log, return
If success: UPDATE status='sent', sent_at=now(), assigned_account_id, message_body
UPDATE wa_accounts SET messages_sent_today += 1. UPDATE campaigns SET sent_count += 1. UPDATE ab_variants SET send_count += 1.
Log error but don't fail the send
* Sends an alert via Discord webhook and/or email.
* Given an experiment, selects a variant using weighted random selection.
// 2. Fetch all variants for this experiment
// 3. If experiment has a winner: always return winner's template
// 4. Else: weighted random based on traffic_split
// Roll a random number 0-100. If < 50, pick A. Else pick B.
Called periodically (every 10 sends). Runs the two-proportion Z-test.
// 1. Fetch all variants with their counts
// 2. If any variant has send_count < min_sample_per_variant: return not significant
// 3. For each pair of variants, compute Z-test:
// 6. If auto_select_winner: UPDATE ab_experiments SET winner_variant_id, status='completed'
Imports fonts (Geist Sans + Geist Mono via next/font). Wraps children in: <ThemeProvider> → <SupabaseProvider> → <Toaster/> → <SidebarLayout>.
Redirect to /dashboard: import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'; redirect('/dashboard');
Full-screen centered login form. Email + password. Calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword(). On success: router.push('/dashboard').
Runs on every request. Checks for Supabase session. If no session AND path !== /login: redirect to /login. If session AND path === /login: redirect to /dashboard.
Server component. Fetches initial KPI data. Renders <DashboardClient> with initialData prop.
'use client'. Receives initialData. Sets up Supabase Realtime subscriptions for live updates. Renders KPI cards, charts, live feed.
Campaign list. DataTable with columns: name, status badge, progress bar, delivery rate, created date, action buttons.
Multi-step campaign creation wizard. 7 steps (see Section 28).
Campaign detail/report. Stats summary + charts + error log + A/B results (if applicable).
Template list with search, filter by category, sort by performance.
Template editor with Spintax syntax highlighting and live preview.
Edit existing template. Same editor as /new but pre-filled.
A/B experiment list. Status badges, winner indicators.
Experiment detail: variant comparison table, significance indicator, real-time chart.
Account command center. 4 cards in a 2x2 grid (responsive: 1 column on mobile).
Contact manager. DataTable + upload modal + bulk actions toolbar.
Segment builder. Visual filter builder + save segment form.
All system config controls. Organized in collapsible sections.
Activity log. Infinite-scroll table of all admin and system events.
createBrowserClient() for client components. Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars.
createServerClient() for server components and API routes. Uses cookies().
createAdminClient() for API routes needing service_role. Bypasses RLS.
useRealtime(table, filter, callback). Auto subscribe/unsubscribe on mount/unmount.
useConfig(). Returns all system_config as a typed object. Updates in real-time.
cn() function (clsx + tailwind-merge). formatPhone(). formatDate(). formatNumber().
TypeScript interfaces for ALL 11 database tables. Used everywhere.
Zod schemas for every form and API request body. Shared validation.
27. Dashboard Page — Every Card, Chart, Action
27.1 KPI Cards (Top Row — 6 Cards in 3x2 Grid)
SUM of sent_count from today's send_logs
COUNT wa_accounts WHERE status=active AND connection_status=connected
Green if 4, Yellow if 3, Red if <3
Total replies / total sent from last 7 days
COUNT send_logs WHERE status=failed AND today
send_logs grouped by hour, today vs yesterday
X-axis: 10AM–10PM. Two bars per hour: today (blue) + yesterday (gray). Tooltip shows exact count.
send_logs grouped by day, last 7 days
Two lines: Sent (blue solid) + Failed (red dashed). Area fill under sent line.
4 segments, one per account. Label with account name + count. Center text: total.
For each active campaign: name + horizontal bar showing sent(green)/pending(gray)/failed(red).
A scrolling list of the last 50 events, updated in real-time via Supabase Realtime subscription on send_logs.

Each row shows: timestamp (relative, e.g., '2s ago'), icon (green check for sent, red X for failed), message: 'Sent to +91 98765XXXXX via Account-B', or 'Failed: connection timeout for +91 12345XXXXX'.
New events animate in from the top with a subtle slide-down animation.
Clicking a row opens a detail panel showing: full phone number, template used, account used, latency, error message (if failed).
PATCH /api/v1/config body: { global_send_enabled: false }. Confirmation dialog first.
Opens modal: phone number input + template selector + send button. Inserts directly into message_queue.
Re-fetches all dashboard data. For when Realtime is slow.
Radio: All Contacts / Select Segment / Upload CSV. If segment: dropdown. If CSV: drag-drop zone.
Radio: Existing Template / New Template. If existing: searchable dropdown with preview. If new: inline template editor.
Toggle: Enable A/B? If yes: select experiment or create new inline. Shows variant summary.
send_window_start (time picker), send_window_end (time picker), daily_limit (slider 50–1000), per_account_limit (slider 25–300)
Full read-only summary of all steps. Estimated completion time. Test Send button (sends to your own number).
A specialized textarea with Spintax awareness. NOT a rich text editor — it edits plain text with visual highlights.

Render the template in a div overlay on top of a transparent textarea. Use regex to wrap {options} in blue <span> and [variables] in green <span>. The textarea captures input; the overlay shows highlights.
Row of buttons above editor: [Name], [Company], [City], [Custom...]. Clicking inserts the placeholder at cursor position in the textarea.
Select text, click 'Add Variants' button. A popover appears with a list input: add alternative phrases. On confirm, wraps selection in {selected|alt1|alt2}.

Right panel (or below on mobile). Shows one randomly resolved version of the template. 'Refresh' button generates another variation. Uses resolveSpintax() from lib (shared code or re-implemented in browser).
Below editor: 'This template produces ~N unique variations'. Calculate: multiply the option counts of each Spintax group.
Show: 'Max message length: 247 chars'. Calculate the longest possible resolved message.
Dropdown above editor: general, promotional, transactional, follow_up, greeting, custom.
POST /api/v1/templates for new, PATCH /api/v1/templates/:id for edit. Show success toast.
Route: /experiments/[id] — The nerve center for a running experiment.
Experiment name, status badge, hypothesis text
DataTable: variant label, template preview (first 80 chars), sends, deliveries, replies, delivery rate (%), reply rate (%), 'Leading' badge on best
For each variant pair: progress bar showing % toward confidence threshold. E.g., 'A vs B: 87% confidence (need 95%)'
Computed from variant counts using Z-test formula
Line chart. X-axis: total sends. Y-axis: cumulative delivery rate per variant. One line per variant.
Realtime (new data points on each send)
Green banner: 'Variant B is the winner with 97.2% confidence!' + 'Apply to Campaign' button
Buttons: 'Declare Winner Manually' (dropdown to pick variant), 'Cancel Experiment', 'Export Results'
Route: /settings — Every control writes to system_config via PATCH /api/v1/config.
Every control is debounced (500ms). When the user stops adjusting, it fires:
PATCH /api/v1/config with body { key: 'xxx', value: newValue }
The API route updates system_config. Supabase Realtime pushes to the worker.
A toast confirms: 'Setting updated'. Worker picks up the change in < 2 seconds.

All /api/v1/* routes require a valid Supabase Auth session. The middleware extracts the JWT from the Authorization header (or cookie), verifies it with supabase.auth.getUser(), and injects the user object into the request.
// Every error response follows this exact shape:
If segment_id provided: query contacts matching segment, insert rows into message_queue with campaign_id and status=pending.
If contact_ids provided: insert rows into message_queue for each contact.
If ab_experiment_id is set: UPDATE ab_experiments SET status = 'running', started_at = now().
The worker will start picking up pending messages for this campaign on its next tick.
Sets campaign status to 'paused'. Worker skips pending messages for paused campaigns.
Creates a new campaign with identical settings but status='draft'. Does NOT copy message_queue rows. Returns the new campaign.
... (all system_config keys as a flat object)
For each update: UPDATE system_config SET value = $1 WHERE key = $2. Insert row into audit_log. Supabase Realtime notifies the worker.
{ sentToday, deliveryRate, activeAccounts, replyRate, pending, failedToday }
Append to live feed. Update KPI cards. Update campaign progress.
Update account cards: status badge, message count, connection status.
Update A/B experiment charts and significance indicators.
Confirm settings saved (toast). Update useConfig hook.
If status changed to 'reconnecting' (from dashboard): trigger reconnect.
If paused: stop processing queue for this campaign. If active: resume.
SSH into your VPS: ssh user@your-vps-ip
Install Node.js 20: curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo -E bash - && sudo apt install -y nodejs
Clone the worker repo: git clone <your-repo-url> wa-worker && cd wa-worker
Create .env file with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
Pair accounts: node src/pair-accounts.js (scan 4 QR codes)
Start with PM2: pm2 start src/index.js --name wa-worker
Enable auto-restart on reboot: pm2 save && pm2 startup
Verify: pm2 logs wa-worker (should show 'Dispatcher started, 4 accounts connected')
Push your Next.js project to GitHub.
Go to vercel.com → New Project → Import your GitHub repo.
Set environment variables in Vercel Dashboard: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY.
Deploy. Vercel auto-builds and deploys on every git push to main.
Optionally: Add custom domain in Vercel Dashboard → Domains.
Use aged numbers (>30 days). Full profiles. Stealth engine with variable timing. Stay under 125/account/day. Have 1–2 backup numbers ready.
Reduce volume immediately. Use completely different numbers. Wait 7 days before re-registering. Consider WhatsApp Business API for legitimate use.
Supabase Auth with strong password. Service role key only on server-side. RLS on all tables. RBAC enforced on every API route.
Worker validates all config values on receive. Invalid values are rejected and logged. Revert to last known good config.
Bonferroni correction for >2 variants. Minimum 50 samples enforced. Manual override available. Clear significance display.
Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff in useRealtime hook. Dashboard polls as fallback every 30s. Worker has local config cache.
PM2 auto-restarts in 5s. Idempotent queue (row-locking) prevents double-sends. Message left in 'processing' is retried after timeout.
Frontend validates phone format before upload. Backend deduplicates. Preview first 10 rows before import. Rollback on bulk error.
Version 3.0 FINAL — 49 Sections — 11 Tables — 60+ API Endpoints
